name: Grain

evidence.claim: The Grain API supports OAuth2 and Personal/Workspace access tokens, authenticated via Bearer headers.
Grain provides an official MCP Server & API.
Search citation: Does Grain Share my Data with 3rd parties like OpenAI? | Grain
Search citation: Grain AI notetaker deep dive: cross-meeting SPICED analysis, HubSpot sync, and coaching workflows

api_notes: The API provides endpoints for core functionality including managing and listing recordings and transcripts. Documentation describes it as a public API supporting Personal and Workspace tokens.
mcp_notes: The official Grain website indicates the availability of an MCP Server & API.
one_liner: Grain is an AI-powered meeting notetaker that records, transcribes, and summarizes meetings to provide actionable insights.
auth_notes: API calls use the Authorization: Bearer TOKEN header. Supported tokens include Personal Access Tokens (PATs), Workspace Access Tokens, and OAuth2 access tokens.
access_notes: Developers can generate Personal Access Tokens (PATs) and Workspace Access Tokens directly from their Grain account settings, or implement the OAuth2 flow for third-party integrations.
